workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Can you extract the partner engagement from this escalation email for Pacific Northwest Outfitters? Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 17:13 EST
Subject: URGENT: partner engagement Issue at Pacific Northwest Outfitters

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 11:45 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Pacific Northwest Outfitters partner engagement

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with partner engagement. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 16:50 EST
Subject: Re: Re: partner engagement Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-M trap (Separation of renewal handling across 4 skills means CSA gets fragmented narrati); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-M failure mode (Separation of renewal handling across 4 skills means CSA gets fragmented narrati)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-M -- Separation of renewal handling across 4 skills means CSA gets fragmented narrati
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
